meta.title: 25 Questions Before Christmas
meta.sub: One question a day through Advent — from the ancient promises to the wonder of “the Word became flesh.” Twenty-five short, fully-cited answers building to Christmas morning. Walk it alone, or with your whole family or church.
meta.completeMsg: From the first promise to “God with us” — you’ve traced why the child in the manger changes everything. Now you can give a reason for the hope you have.
meta.shareText: A 25-day Advent journey — one question a day, from the prophecies to the Incarnation, building to Christmas. Walk it with me?
meta.seasonNote: Made for Advent (Dec 1–25), but you can start any day.
meta.kitLede: Built for a family, a small group, or a whole church to walk together through Advent. Share the link, or print the full 25-question list for an apologetics Advent calendar.

movements.s: Days 6–10 · Can we trust the story?

days.q: Did Jesus really exist?
days.why: Before the manger scene — did the child of Christmas actually live?

days.q: How many Old Testament prophecies did Jesus fulfill?
days.why: Centuries of promises, all pointing forward to one coming.

days.q: Did Jesus fulfill Isaiah 53?
days.why: The servant’s portrait, written 700 years before Bethlehem.

days.q: What is the Daniel 70 weeks prophecy?
days.why: Daniel’s startling countdown to the Messiah’s arrival.

days.q: Is the Trinity in the Old Testament?
days.why: The Son was never absent — present long before the manger.

days.q: Were the Gospels written by eyewitnesses?
days.why: Is the Christmas story memory, or later legend?

days.q: When were the gospels written?
days.why: Early enough that the first witnesses could still check it.

days.q: Is there archaeological evidence for the Bible?
days.why: Bethlehem, Nazareth, the census world — does the spade agree?

days.q: Is the virgin birth credible?
days.why: The sign of Immanuel: can it be taken seriously?

days.q: Was Jesus copied from pagan myths like Horus and Mithras?
days.why: The “December 25 / Horus” objection, faced squarely.

days.q: Did Jesus actually claim to be God?
days.why: Did the baby of Bethlehem grow up to claim it himself?

days.q: What is the evidence that Jesus is God?
days.why: The case for the child’s divine identity, laid out plainly.

days.q: Why is Jesus called the Son of God?
days.why: What the title means — and what it does not.

days.q: Wasn't Jesus just a good moral teacher?
days.why: The one thing a merely-good teacher can’t be.

days.q: Is Jesus God, or just a prophet?
days.why: A prophet points to God. This child is worshipped as God.

days.q: Isn't the Trinity a contradiction?
days.why: How the one God can come to us as the Son.

days.q: Where is the Trinity in the Bible?
days.why: Not a later invention — woven through the text.

days.q: What does 'Son of Man' mean?
days.why: The humble title that is quietly a claim to heaven’s throne.

days.q: Was Jesus sinless?
days.why: The spotless life the manger was heading toward.

days.q: Did Jesus empty himself of his deity? (Philippians 2)
days.why: Philippians 2: how God could become a baby without ceasing to be God.

days.q: What makes Christianity different from other religions?
days.why: Every faith reaches up. Only here does God come down.

days.q: Do Muslims and Christians worship the same God?
days.why: Islam honours Jesus but denies the Incarnation. Shared words, not shared belief.

days.q: If God is good, why is there so much suffering?
days.why: Immanuel — “God with us” — means he entered the ache himself.

days.q: What about those who have never heard of Jesus?
days.why: The light of Christmas was always meant for every nation.

days.q: Is there evidence that Jesus rose from the dead?
days.why: Christmas Day. The child in the manger was born for the empty tomb.
